purpose: Repeatable DigestSnap scan trust checks for food identity, score, confidence source, nutrition, and correction behavior.

rules.passDefinition: A scan passes when product identity is understandable, rating is in the expected band, confidence source matches the image type, and nutrition is plausible for one serving or one package.
doNotPass: Sugary soda or iced tea scored above 55
Whole fruit scored below 75 without a user trigger
Correction only changes UI and is not saved to scan_corrections
Russian/Cyrillic product name returns an English-only error when the app language is Russian

cases.input: Photo of one clear apple, no label
cases.notes: No cleaner alternative should be shown for a high-score whole fruit.

cases.input: Photo of one avocado or sliced avocado
cases.notes: Should not become unreadable just because there is no label.

cases.input: Photo of boiled or plain cooked eggs
Clear front label photo of Borjomi mineral water
Clear Coca-Cola can or bottle label
cases.notes: Database nutrition must not override soda risk rules.

cases.input: Clear Kinder Молочный ломтик wrapper with Cyrillic text
cases.notes: Must handle Cyrillic text and brand recognition.

cases.input: Photo of cooked macaroni or pasta without a package
Blurry packaged food where brand and food type are not identifiable
cases.notes: Saved image is allowed, but confidence must say needs confirmation and not Safe.

cases.input: Open a scan result, tap Fix result, change score and nutrition, save
cases.databaseExpectation: scan_corrections row upserted with same user_id and local_scan_id
cases.notes: Correction should update current modal, recent upload card, local cache, and Supabase row.
